header: Gerente de Excelência Comercial — Sanofi Medley

lesson.menu_desc: Review of the Experiences block -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 20 -- Checkpoint 4 -- present perfect, past simple, for & since, interviews
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Gleice Leonardo Rocha de Souza | Aula 20 -- Checkpoint 4 -- The Full Story

listenings.text: Good morning, Gleice, and welcome to our final review of the block. Let me give you a quick recap. This block was all about your experiences and your career story. You have learned to talk about your life with the present perfect: I have visited many countries, and I have worked in this field for many years. You have also used the past simple for finished moments: I started here in 2012, and I joined the team last year. You have learned to give a duration with for and a start point with since: I have been a manager for six years, and I have led this team since 2018. And finally, you have practiced a job interview: tell me about yourself, what is your biggest strength, and why do you want this job. You have done excellent work, and you are a strong candidate. Congratulations on finishing the block.

Hello, my name is Renata, and I want to share my experiences. I have worked in the pharmaceutical field for fifteen years, and I have lived in São Paulo since 2010. I started my career as a junior, and I joined this company in 2015. My background is in biology, and my biggest strength is communication. I have visited many clients, and I have learned a new skill every year. In my last interview, I was calm, honest, and confident. I have grown a lot, and I am very proud of my career story. How long have you worked in your field, and what is your biggest strength?

extra_audio.text: First, you talk about a life experience with the present perfect: I have visited many countries. Then you give a finished moment with the past simple: I started this job in 2012. After that, you give a duration with for: I have worked here for ten years. Next, you give a start point with since: I have been a manager since 2018. Finally, you answer an interview question: my biggest strength is communication, and I am a strong candidate.
